_comment: GM VIP Automation Framework – test case definitions.
_comment2: Edit this file to add/change test cases, expected values, and CAPL references.
_comment3: Pass the path to this file to runner.run_from_json() – no Python changes needed.
_comment4: Key reference: enabled, reset_before, go_before_check, breakpoints, variables_write, variables_check, symbols_inspect.
_comment5: go_before_check=true: after variable writes + breakpoint setup, issue GO and wait for halt before reading variables. Use when variables are only valid after the ECU has run its init code.

test_cases._comment: --- Test case 1: Reset and basic ECU state check ---
test_cases.name: TC_Reset_BasicState

myModule.myStatus.description: Read status after reset – no assertion, just log

test_cases._comment: --- Test case 2: Breakpoint + pre-condition write + post-halt variable checks ---
test_cases.name: TC_Breakpoint_VarCheck

myModule.myFlag.description: Set flag before GO – mirrors A_DBGR_VariableWrite in CAPL

myModule.myCounter.description: Read counter after halt – no assertion, value logged in report

myModule.myStatus.description: Status must equal 0x01 after myTargetFunction runs

test_cases._comment: --- Test case 3: go_before_check example (no explicit breakpoint – ECU runs until natural halt) ---
test_cases._comment2: Use this pattern when variables only have expected values AFTER the ECU has executed its init code.
test_cases.name: TC_GoBeforeCheck_Example

myModule.myFlag.description: Set flag, then GO so the ECU processes it

myModule.myStatus.description: Status after ECU init – checked after ECU halts
